message:
fix(beads): correct --label design to match owner-approved GetProjectDir stripping

CRITICAL DESIGN REVISION: The original beads incorrectly stated "Do NOT
modify GetProjectDir." The owner explicitly approved GetProjectDir
label-stripping in GH issue #49 comment: "GetProjectDir label-stripping
approach is clean and minimal."

The design is now corrected: GetProjectDir strips labels via SessionBase(),
so myproject--frontend and myproject--backend both resolve to
~/projects/myproject/. Multiple labeled sessions share the SAME project
directory, which is the entire point -- different agent swarms working on
the same codebase.

Changes to beads:
- Epic description rewritten for shared-directory design
- Task .1 (design) corrected with GetProjectDir-stripping as core principle
- NEW task .13: Modify GetProjectDir (the actual core one-line change)
- Task .4 (spawn) updated: project dir is base dir, not labeled dir
- Task .8 (inferSessionFromCWD) rewritten: now needs REAL disambiguation
  logic with three-tier approach (prefer unlabeled > error with options)
- NEW task .14: --project filter for list/send/kill (UX improvement)
- NEW task .15: Verify checkpoint/Agent Mail/CASS with shared dirs
- Tasks .5, .6, .7, .9, .10, .11, .12 updated for shared-dir behavior
- E2E test .10: fixed critical assertion (dirs SAME, not different)
- Dependency graph expanded: 36 edges across 16 nodes, zero cycles
- Corrective comments added to tasks .1, .4, .8, and epic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
